forecast-period.text: A low pressure system is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA today. The low will then move over Bass Strait later today. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday. A high pressure system will become established south of WA on Thursday and will extend a ridge over western SA.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district, possibly extending into the south of the pastoral districts. Showers mostly contracting to coastal districts during the evening. Possible thunderstorms about eastern and central districts south of about Adelaide, clearing by early evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwesterly winds extending throughout, strong and gusty at times near southern coasts.
Isolated showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches in the west. Isolated showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district, more frequent about the southern agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds turning northwesterly in the south ahead of a fresh and gusty southerly change late afternoon and evening, strong near southern coasts.

text.p: Isolated showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district, more frequent near southern and far western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: 5 to 15 mm about the southern agricultural area and near western coasts, locally reaching 15 to 25 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges, the Lower South East and western Kangaroo Island. Isolated falls of 25 to 40 mm are possible about the Mount Lofty Ranges. 2 to 10 mm is expected over the remainder of the agricultural area and 2 to 5 mm about the remainder of the West Coast district. Less than 2 mm is expected over the south of the pastoral districts, but 2 to 10 mm is expected in the far northeast. Isolated higher falls are possible with thunderstorms.
